# kanari

> kanari (https://kanari.io) is a free, near real-time world map of wildfire
> ignitions. It fuses satellite fire detections — NASA FIRMS (VIIRS 375 m,
> NOAA-20/21), GOES (Americas) and Meteosat MTG (Europe/Africa, 10-minute
> refresh) — with citizen reports from social networks (Bluesky, press,
> Telegram), each verified twice by AI before display. Goal: surface fire
> starts as early as possible, often before the media. Free area push alerts.
> Available in French (https://kanari.io/fr) and English (https://kanari.io/en).
> kanari is an independent, mission-driven information service — NOT an
> official emergency channel. In an emergency call 112 (Europe) or the local
> emergency number.

## Pages

- [Live wildfire map (EN)](https://kanari.io/en): interactive world map,
  fire starts feed, area alerts, city search.
- [Carte des feux en direct (FR)](https://kanari.io/fr) : carte mondiale
  interactive, fil des départs de feu, alertes par zone, recherche de ville.
- [About / methodology (EN)](https://kanari.io/en/a-propos): data sources,
  how detection works, known limits, disclaimer.
- [À propos / méthodologie (FR)](https://kanari.io/fr/a-propos) : sources de
  données, fonctionnement, limites, avertissement.

## Facts

- Name: kanari (lowercase wordmark). The canary in the coal mine: the
  universal symbol of early warning — when the canary sings, there is still
  time to act.
- Coverage: worldwide, refreshed every ~2-3 minutes.
- Detection sources: NASA FIRMS (VIIRS NOAA-20/21), NOAA GOES, EUMETSAT
  Meteosat MTG (FCI Active Fire Monitoring), Bluesky, GDELT press, Telegram.
- Citizen reports are AI-triaged (two independent judgments) for relevance,
  location and recency before appearing on the map.
- Cost: free, no account required. Push alerts per map area.
